scope: hook-interface-manifest — This manifest defines hook interfaces and the scryrs record invocation contract. It is not a tool catalog, MCP descriptor, agent-callable surface, or tool-output rewriter.

_anti_patterns._comment: The following are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this manifest.
_anti_patterns.NOT_mcp_descriptor: This manifest does not define MCP methods, tools/capabilities, server descriptors, or agent-callable surfaces.
_anti_patterns.NOT_tool_catalog: This manifest does not register or catalog tools for agent consumption. Intercepted tool names are observational metadata describing what the hooks observe.
_anti_patterns.NOT_agent_callable: scryrs is not callable as an agent tool. Do not model it as a tool the agent can invoke.
_anti_patterns.NOT_tool_output_rewriter: This manifest does not describe tool output rewriting, augmentation, or modification.

record.description: Canonical hook ingestion path. Hooks pipe JSONL TraceEvent envelopes to scryrs record --stdin via stdin.

alternateMode.description: Available alternate ingestion mode for reading trace events from a file instead of stdin. Hooks currently use --stdin.

outputContract.stderr: Rejection diagnostics — one JSON object per rejected non-empty line, with line, field, and reason fields.

exitCodes.1: One or more events rejected (partial acceptance), or I/O error.
exitCodes.2: Fatal usage or I/O error (mutually exclusive modes, unreadable file, store failure).

supported_harnesses._interceptedToolsNote: Observational metadata — these are tool names observed by the hook, not a callable surface. debugOnlyInterceptedTools are only captured when SCRYRS_DEBUG is set to a non-empty value.
supported_harnesses.limitations: PreToolUse-only hook. Returns { continue: true } unconditionally (Success outcome). No SessionStart, SessionEnd, PostToolUse, or notification-level events are captured.

supported_harnesses._interceptedToolsNote: Observational metadata — these are tool names observed by the hook, not a callable surface. debugOnlyInterceptedTools are only captured when SCRYRS_DEBUG is set to a non-empty value.
supported_harnesses.limitations: SessionEnd is deferred. Pi's session_shutdown event fires after the hook runtime has already torn down, so SessionEnd events cannot be captured at this time.
